steps.title: Set up
steps.content:
Square both sides of $\sqrt{k-x} = 32 - x$ (valid when $32 - x \ge 0$):
$$k - x = (32-x)^2 = 1024 - 64x + x^2$$
Rearrange: $x^2 - 63x + (1024 - k) = 0$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content:
For the equation to have exactly one valid solution, the discriminant equals zero:
$$\Delta = 63^2 - 4(1024 - k) = 3969 - 4096 + 4k = 4k - 127 = 0$$
$$4k = 127$$
The repeated root $x = \tfrac{63}{2} = 31.5 \le 32$, so it is valid.
